steps.title: Identify the y-intercept and slope from the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at $y = 1.5$ and descends from left to right, indicating a negative slope.

steps.title: Check which form produces the correct slope
steps.content:
Rearrange each option to slope-intercept form and check the slope sign:

Option A ($Rx + 12y = 18$): $12y = -Rx + 18 \Rightarrow y = -\frac{R}{12}x + 1.5$. Since $R > 0$, the slope $-\frac{R}{12}$ is negative. ✓

Option B ($Rx - 12y = -18$): $12y = Rx + 18 \Rightarrow y = \frac{R}{12}x + 1.5$. Since $R > 0$, the slope $\frac{R}{12}$ is positive. ✗

The graph shows a negative slope, matching option A. That matches choice A — A is correct.
